chunks.text: Page 1 THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Present: Mr. Justice Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry Mr. Justice Muhammad Jaffer Raza C.P. No. D-4292 of 2025 [Muhammad Kamran Pirzada v. Chief Selection Committee High Court of Sindh & Others] Petitioner: Muhammad Kamran Pirzada is present in person. Respondents: Nemo. Date of hearing: 01-09-2025 Date of order: 01-09-2025 O R D E R Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry, J. - The Petitioner applied for the post of Junior Office Associate in the High Court of Sindh and passed the entry written test held by SIBA Testing Services (Respondent No.2) on 15.02.2025. He alleges that he was still not called by SIBA Testing Services for the second stage viz. the typing test subsequently held, and consequently, his name does not appear in the list of candidates who are being presently interviewed by the Selection Committee of the High Court. He has pleaded that in that regard he made a complaint to the Registrar High Court which was put up before the Chairman of the Selection Committee (Respondent No.1) but remains unaddressed. He prays for a writ to the Chairman Selection Committee High Court to stop the interview process of other candidates and to afford him a typing test. Since the Petitioner submitted that his complaint was taken up by the Administration of the High Court, we inquired from the Administration Branch about the fate of such complaint. We are informed that upon orders of the Chairman Selection Committee (Respondent No.1), the Petitioner’s contention that he was not informed of the typing test, was duly enquired but was found to be Page 2 without force. Thus, the Petitioner’s complaint has already been examined and by the Chairman Selection Committee who is a Judge of the High Court.

We are informed that upon orders of the Chairman Selection Committee (Respondent No.1), the Petitioner’s contention that he was not informed of the typing test, was duly enquired but was found to be Page 2 without force. Thus, the Petitioner’s complaint has already been examined and by the Chairman Selection Committee who is a Judge of the High Court. It has been settled by the Supreme Court in Gul Taiz Khan Marwat v. Registrar Peshawar High Court (PLD 2021 SC 391) that administrative orders of a High Court Judge are not amenable to writ jurisdiction. Therefore, the petition is dismissed in limine. JUDGE JUDGE *PS/SADAM
